examples.title: Pseudocode: Intensity Slicing
examples.description: Divide grayscale range into intervals and assign specific color to each interval
examples.code:
ALGORITHM IntensitySlicing
INPUT: grayscaleImage, intervalCount
OUTPUT: pseudocolorImage

BEGIN
 FOR EACH pixel IN grayscaleImage
 intensity = GET_INTENSITY(pixel)
 intervalIndex = FLOOR(intensity / (255 / intervalCount))
 color = LOOKUP_COLOR(intervalIndex)
 ASSIGN_COLOR(pixel, color)
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the intensity slicing technique, where the grayscale range is divided into intervals and each interval is assigned a specific color.

examples.title: Python: Lookup Table Implementation
examples.description: Implement pseudocolor processing using a lookup table
examples.code:
def create_lookup_table(interval_count):
 lookup_table = []
 for i in range(256):
 interval_index = i // (256 // interval_count)
 color = (interval_index * 255 // interval_count, 0, 0) # Red color for demonstration
 lookup_table.append(color)
 return lookup_table

def apply_lookup_table(grayscale_image, lookup_table):
 pseudocolor_image = []
 for pixel in grayscale_image:
 intensity = pixel[0] # Assuming grayscale image with single channel
 color = lookup_table[intensity]
 pseudocolor_image.append(color)
 return pseudocolor_image
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates the lookup table implementation for pseudocolor processing. It creates a lookup table with a specified number of intervals and applies it to a grayscale image to produce a pseudocolor image.

examples.title: C: Transformation Functions
examples.description: Implement pseudocolor processing using transformation functions
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates the transformation functions approach to pseudocolor processing. It defines separate transformation functions for each color component and applies them to a grayscale image to produce a pseudocolor image.
